items.headline: City releases children and youth services master plan update
items.summary: San José presented a status report on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan on April 9th, covering multiple youth programs including the San José Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative Grant. This plan guides the city's investment in services and programs for young residents.

items.headline: Planning hearing delayed for 333 Franklin Street project
items.summary: An administrative zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 has been postponed. The project at 333 Franklin Street will be re-noticed with a new hearing date to be determined.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue highway-rail traffic signal project moves forward
items.summary: The City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7th. This ongoing infrastructure project addresses traffic safety at the intersection of a major highway and railroad.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including revised criteria for evaluating projects. This plan will shape how the city prioritizes biking, walking, and transit improvements over the coming years.

items.headline: 65-unit senior affordable housing complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium is developing a major $8.8M senior apartment building with 65 units at 678 E St John St, addressing San José's critical shortage of affordable senior housing. This represents significant new housing supply in a demographic segment facing severe affordability challenges.

items.headline: City Council adopts emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: San José City Council approved a new emergency interim housing siting policy on April 1st. This policy change affects where and how the city can rapidly establish temporary housing solutions, potentially impacting neighborhood zoning and housing availability during emergencies.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition lawsuit against city continues in court
items.summary: The city is in active litigation with the Garden Gate Coalition over Mary Avenue safety concerns. This case involves disputes about how the city addresses traffic and pedestrian safety on a major residential street.

items.headline: City Council approves gas rate changes for 2027
items.summary: Palo Alto is updating its natural gas rates for residential and commercial customers starting in Fiscal Year 2027. The utility rates for gas service (G-1, G-2, and G-3 categories) are being amended, which will affect what residents and businesses pay for gas.
